channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Electrical Switching of the Berry Phase in Bernal Bilayer Graphene Quantum Dots
item.description:
Author(s): Ke Lv, Qiang Cheng, Yu-Chen Zhuang, Chen-Yue Hao, Xiao-Ya Wang, Ya-Xin Zhao, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Ya-Ning Ren, Qing-Feng Sun, and Lin He

The Berry phase—a fundamental quantum geometric phase given by the integral of the Berry curvature over a closed trajectory in momentum space—underlies a wide range of quantum topological phenomena. Recent advances have demonstrated the potential of tuning the Berry phase to access exotic quantum st…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116201] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ke Lv, Qiang Cheng, Yu-Chen Zhuang, Chen-Yue Hao, Xiao-Ya Wang, Ya-Xin Zhao, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Ya-Ning Ren, Qing-Feng Sun, and Lin He

The Berry phase—a fundamental quantum geometric phase given by the integral of the Berry curvature over a closed trajectory in momentum space—underlies a wide range of quantum topological phenomena. Recent advances have demonstrated the potential of tuning the Berry phase to access exotic quantum st…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116201] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Electrical Switching of the Berry Phase in Bernal Bilayer Graphene Quantum Dots
item.dc:creator: Ke Lv, Qiang Cheng, Yu-Chen Zhuang, Chen-Yue Hao, Xiao-Ya Wang, Ya-Xin Zhao, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Ya-Ning Ren, Qing-Feng Sun, and Lin He
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Incompressible Quantum Hall Liquid on the Four-Dimensional Sphere
item.description:
Author(s): Junwen Zhao, Xue Meng, Wei Zhu, and Congjun Wu

The quantum Hall effect (QHE) is a cornerstone of topological physics, inspiring extensive explorations of its high-dimensional generalizations such as experimental realizations in synthetic systems including cold atoms, photonic lattices, and metamaterials. However, the many-body effect in the high…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116501] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Junwen Zhao, Xue Meng, Wei Zhu, and Congjun Wu

The quantum Hall effect (QHE) is a cornerstone of topological physics, inspiring extensive explorations of its high-dimensional generalizations such as experimental realizations in synthetic systems including cold atoms, photonic lattices, and metamaterials. However, the many-body effect in the high…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116501] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Incompressible Quantum Hall Liquid on the Four-Dimensional Sphere
item.dc:creator: Junwen Zhao, Xue Meng, Wei Zhu, and Congjun Wu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Non-Hermiticity Induced Universal Anomalies in Kondo Conductance
item.description:
Author(s): Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang

Strong correlation, when combined with dissipation in open systems, can lead to a variety of exotic quantum phenomena. Here, we study nontrivial interplays between non-Fermi liquid behaviors emerging from strong correlation and non-Hermiticity arising from open systems. We propose a practical physic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116502] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang

Strong correlation, when combined with dissipation in open systems, can lead to a variety of exotic quantum phenomena. Here, we study nontrivial interplays between non-Fermi liquid behaviors emerging from strong correlation and non-Hermiticity arising from open systems. We propose a practical physic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116502] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Non-Hermiticity Induced Universal Anomalies in Kondo Conductance
item.dc:creator: Wei-Zhu Yi, Yun Chen, Jun-Jun Pang, Hong Chen, Baigeng Wang, and Rui Wang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Isoperimetric Inequalities in Quantum Geometry
item.description:
Author(s): Praveen Pai and Fan Zhang

The Berry phase and quantum distance characterize the geometry of wave functions in Hilbert space. We uncover a quantum analog of the classical isoperimetric inequality, revealing a fundamental link between the Berry phase and quantum distance along closed paths. For two-band systems, we establish a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116601] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Praveen Pai and Fan Zhang

The Berry phase and quantum distance characterize the geometry of wave functions in Hilbert space. We uncover a quantum analog of the classical isoperimetric inequality, revealing a fundamental link between the Berry phase and quantum distance along closed paths. For two-band systems, we establish a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116601] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Isoperimetric Inequalities in Quantum Geometry
item.dc:creator: Praveen Pai and Fan Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum Geometric Inequality and Its Classical Wave Verification
item.description:
Author(s): Tingzhi Liu (刘亭志), Qicheng Zhang (张起成), and Chunyin Qiu (邱春印)

The study of the geometric properties of quantum states in Hilbert space—particularly through the lens of the quantum geometric tensor (QGT)—has profoundly advanced the fields of condensed matter physics and materials science. The real and imaginary parts of the QGT, the quantum metric and Berry cur…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116602] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tingzhi Liu (刘亭志), Qicheng Zhang (张起成), and Chunyin Qiu (邱春印)

The study of the geometric properties of quantum states in Hilbert space—particularly through the lens of the quantum geometric tensor (QGT)—has profoundly advanced the fields of condensed matter physics and materials science. The real and imaginary parts of the QGT, the quantum metric and Berry cur…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116602] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Geometric Inequality and Its Classical Wave Verification
item.dc:creator: Tingzhi Liu (刘亭志), Qicheng Zhang (张起成), and Chunyin Qiu (邱春印)
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Spin-Polarized Josephson Supercurrent in Nodeless Altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Chuang Li, Jin-Xing Hou, Fu-Chun Zhang, Song-Bo Zhang, and Lun-Hui Hu

Long-range propagation of equal-spin triplet Cooper pairs typically occurs in ferromagnet-$s$-wave superconductor junctions, where net magnetization plays a crucial role. Here, we propose a fundamentally different scenario in which Josephson supercurrents mediated exclusively by spin-triplet pairing…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116701] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chuang Li, Jin-Xing Hou, Fu-Chun Zhang, Song-Bo Zhang, and Lun-Hui Hu

Long-range propagation of equal-spin triplet Cooper pairs typically occurs in ferromagnet-s-wave superconductor junctions, where net magnetization plays a crucial role. Here, we propose a fundamentally different scenario in which Josephson supercurrents mediated exclusively by spin-triplet pairings …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116701] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-Polarized Josephson Supercurrent in Nodeless Altermagnets
item.dc:creator: Chuang Li, Jin-Xing Hou, Fu-Chun Zhang, Song-Bo Zhang, and Lun-Hui Hu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Unified Description of Spin-Lattice Coupling and Thermodynamics in the Pyrochlore Heisenberg Antiferromagnet
item.description:
Author(s): Masaki Gen, Hidemaro Suwa, Shusaku Imajo, Chao Dong, Hiroaki Ueda, Makoto Tachibana, Akihiko Ikeda, Koichi Kindo, and Yoshimitsu Kohama

We study an extended model to describe the spin-lattice coupling, incorporating individual vibrations of bonds and atomic sites alongside distance-dependent exchange interactions. The proposed spin Hamiltonian can be effectively considered as an interpolation between two well-established minimum mod…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116702] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Masaki Gen, Hidemaro Suwa, Shusaku Imajo, Chao Dong, Hiroaki Ueda, Makoto Tachibana, Akihiko Ikeda, Koichi Kindo, and Yoshimitsu Kohama

We study an extended model to describe the spin-lattice coupling, incorporating individual vibrations of bonds and atomic sites alongside distance-dependent exchange interactions. The proposed spin Hamiltonian can be effectively considered as an interpolation between two well-established minimum mod…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116702] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Unified Description of Spin-Lattice Coupling and Thermodynamics in the Pyrochlore Heisenberg Antiferromagnet
item.dc:creator: Masaki Gen, Hidemaro Suwa, Shusaku Imajo, Chao Dong, Hiroaki Ueda, Makoto Tachibana, Akihiko Ikeda, Koichi Kindo, and Yoshimitsu Kohama
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Anisotropic Upper Critical Field beyond the Pauli Limit in a Nickelate Superconductor: Evidence for a Quantum Fluctuation Driven State
item.description:
Author(s): Xue-Yan Wang, Cheng-Xue Chen, Zi-Ming Mai, Qiang Zhao, Wen-Long Yang, Fang-Hui Zhu, Mei-Ling Yan, Rui-Fen Dou, Chang-Min Xiong, Haiwen Liu, and Jia-Cai Nie

High-field magnetotransport measurements of a nickelate superconductor up to 44 T reveal an anisotropic quantum Griffiths singularity, establishing disorder-enhanced quantum fluctuations as an intrinsic mechanism for protecting superconductivity.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106002] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xue-Yan Wang, Cheng-Xue Chen, Zi-Ming Mai, Qiang Zhao, Wen-Long Yang, Fang-Hui Zhu, Mei-Ling Yan, Rui-Fen Dou, Chang-Min Xiong, Haiwen Liu, and Jia-Cai Nie

High-field magnetotransport measurements of a nickelate superconductor up to 44 T reveal an anisotropic quantum Griffiths singularity, establishing disorder-enhanced quantum fluctuations as an intrinsic mechanism for protecting superconductivity.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106002] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Anisotropic Upper Critical Field beyond the Pauli Limit in a Nickelate Superconductor: Evidence for a Quantum Fluctuation Driven State
item.dc:creator: Xue-Yan Wang, Cheng-Xue Chen, Zi-Ming Mai, Qiang Zhao, Wen-Long Yang, Fang-Hui Zhu, Mei-Ling Yan, Rui-Fen Dou, Chang-Min Xiong, Haiwen Liu, and Jia-Cai Nie
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Interlayer Exciton Condensates between Second Landau Level Orbitals in Double Bilayer Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Zeyu Hao, A. M. Zimmerman, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, and Philip Kim

Using Coulomb drag as an unambiguous probe of interlayer correlation reveals the first direct quantized drag evidence of interlayer exciton condensation beyond N = 0 in double bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106505] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zeyu Hao, A. M. Zimmerman, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, and Philip Kim

Using Coulomb drag as an unambiguous probe of interlayer correlation reveals the first direct quantized drag evidence of interlayer exciton condensation beyond N = 0 in double bilayer graphene.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106505] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Interlayer Exciton Condensates between Second Landau Level Orbitals in Double Bilayer Graphene
item.dc:creator: Zeyu Hao, A. M. Zimmerman, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, and Philip Kim
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Algebraic Loop Liquid in the Pyrochlore ${\mathrm{CsNiCrF}}_{6}$
item.description:
Author(s): A. Hemmatzade, C. Balz, J. Ollivier, E. Lhotel, N. Colonna, A. dos Santos Rufino, G. J. Nilsen, Ø. S. Fjellvåg, M. Medarde, S. T. Bramwell, M. J. Harris, P. M. Derlet, and T. Fennell

The $β$-pyrochlore ${\mathrm{CsNiCrF}}_{6}$ is a charge ice—each tetrahedron of the pyrochlore lattice is occupied by two ${\mathrm{Ni}}^{2+}$ and two ${\mathrm{Cr}}^{3+}$ according to an ice rule, resulting in fully packed same-species loops with power-law length distribution. Using inelastic neutr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106701] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. Hemmatzade, C. Balz, J. Ollivier, E. Lhotel, N. Colonna, A. dos Santos Rufino, G. J. Nilsen, Ø. S. Fjellvåg, M. Medarde, S. T. Bramwell, M. J. Harris, P. M. Derlet, and T. Fennell

The β-pyrochlore CsNiCrF6 is a charge ice—each tetrahedron of the pyrochlore lattice is occupied by two Ni2+ and two Cr3+ according to an ice rule, resulting in fully packed same-species loops with power-law length distribution. Using inelastic neutron scattering and numerical simulations, we show t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106701] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Algebraic Loop Liquid in the Pyrochlore ${\mathrm{CsNiCrF}}_{6}$
item.dc:creator: A. Hemmatzade, C. Balz, J. Ollivier, E. Lhotel, N. Colonna, A. dos Santos Rufino, G. J. Nilsen, Ø. S. Fjellvåg, M. Medarde, S. T. Bramwell, M. J. Harris, P. M. Derlet, and T. Fennell
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Nonlocal Correlation Effects in dc and Optical Conductivity of the Hubbard Model
item.description:
Author(s): Nagamalleswararao Dasari, Hugo U. R. Strand, Martin Eckstein, Alexander I. Lichtenstein, and Evgeny A. Stepanov

Conductivity is one of the most direct probes of electronic systems, yet its theoretical description remains challenging in the presence of strong nonlocal correlations. In this Letter, we analyze the conductivity of the half-filled single-band Hubbard model and identify the role of spatial correlat…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106905] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nagamalleswararao Dasari, Hugo U. R. Strand, Martin Eckstein, Alexander I. Lichtenstein, and Evgeny A. Stepanov

Conductivity is one of the most direct probes of electronic systems, yet its theoretical description remains challenging in the presence of strong nonlocal correlations. In this Letter, we analyze the conductivity of the half-filled single-band Hubbard model and identify the role of spatial correlat…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106905] Published Fri Mar 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Nonlocal Correlation Effects in dc and Optical Conductivity of the Hubbard Model
item.dc:creator: Nagamalleswararao Dasari, Hugo U. R. Strand, Martin Eckstein, Alexander I. Lichtenstein, and Evgeny A. Stepanov
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Geometrical Frustration, Power Law Tunneling and Nonlocal Gauge Fields from Scattered Light
item.description:
Author(s): Pavel P. Popov, Joana Fraxanet, Luca Barbiero, and Maciej Lewenstein

Designing the amplitude and range of couplings in quantum systems is a fundamental tool for exploring a large variety of quantum mechanical effects. Here, we consider off-resonant photon scattering processes on a geometrically shaped molecular cloud. Our analysis shows that such a setup is properly …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 103403] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pavel P. Popov, Joana Fraxanet, Luca Barbiero, and Maciej Lewenstein

Designing the amplitude and range of couplings in quantum systems is a fundamental tool for exploring a large variety of quantum mechanical effects. Here, we consider off-resonant photon scattering processes on a geometrically shaped molecular cloud. Our analysis shows that such a setup is properly …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 103403] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Geometrical Frustration, Power Law Tunneling and Nonlocal Gauge Fields from Scattered Light
item.dc:creator: Pavel P. Popov, Joana Fraxanet, Luca Barbiero, and Maciej Lewenstein
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Implementing Non-Abelian Hatano-Nelson Model in Electric Circuits
item.description:
Author(s): Xiangru Chen, Jien Wu, Xingyu Chen, Zhenhang Pu, Yejian Hu, Jiuyang Lu, Manzhu Ke, Weiyin Deng, and Zhengyou Liu

Non-Hermitian systems generally host complex spectra that bring unique spectral topologies, leading to spectral braiding and the non-Hermitian skin effect. The experimental exploration of non-Hermitian physics is mainly concentrated in artificial systems, due to the flexibility in the introduction o…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106604] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiangru Chen, Jien Wu, Xingyu Chen, Zhenhang Pu, Yejian Hu, Jiuyang Lu, Manzhu Ke, Weiyin Deng, and Zhengyou Liu

Non-Hermitian systems generally host complex spectra that bring unique spectral topologies, leading to spectral braiding and the non-Hermitian skin effect. The experimental exploration of non-Hermitian physics is mainly concentrated in artificial systems, due to the flexibility in the introduction o…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106604] Published Thu Mar 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Implementing Non-Abelian Hatano-Nelson Model in Electric Circuits
item.dc:creator: Xiangru Chen, Jien Wu, Xingyu Chen, Zhenhang Pu, Yejian Hu, Jiuyang Lu, Manzhu Ke, Weiyin Deng, and Zhengyou Liu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Designing Lattice Spin Models and Magnon Gaps with Supercurrents
item.description:
Author(s): Johanne Bratland Tjernshaugen, Martin Tang Bruland, and Jacob Linder

Electric control over magnetic interactions at the level of individual spins is relevant for a variety of quantum applications, such as qubits, memory, and sensor functionality. We show here that spin lattices and magnon gaps can be controlled with a supercurrent. Remarkably, a spin-polarized superc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106001] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Johanne Bratland Tjernshaugen, Martin Tang Bruland, and Jacob Linder

Electric control over magnetic interactions at the level of individual spins is relevant for a variety of quantum applications, such as qubits, memory, and sensor functionality. We show here that spin lattices and magnon gaps can be controlled with a supercurrent. Remarkably, a spin-polarized superc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106001] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Designing Lattice Spin Models and Magnon Gaps with Supercurrents
item.dc:creator: Johanne Bratland Tjernshaugen, Martin Tang Bruland, and Jacob Linder
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Altermagnetic Spin Precession and Spin Transistor
item.description:
Author(s): Li-Shuo Liu, Kai Shao, Hai-Dong Li, Xiangang Wan, Wei Chen, and D. Y. Xing

Altermagnets hold great potential for spintronic applications, yet their intrinsic spin dynamics and associated transport properties remain largely unexplored. Here, we investigate spin-resolved quantum transport in a multiterminal setup based on a $d$-wave altermagnet. It is found that the altermag…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106301] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Li-Shuo Liu, Kai Shao, Hai-Dong Li, Xiangang Wan, Wei Chen, and D. Y. Xing

Altermagnets hold great potential for spintronic applications, yet their intrinsic spin dynamics and associated transport properties remain largely unexplored. Here, we investigate spin-resolved quantum transport in a multiterminal setup based on a d-wave altermagnet. It is found that the altermagne…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106301] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Altermagnetic Spin Precession and Spin Transistor
item.dc:creator: Li-Shuo Liu, Kai Shao, Hai-Dong Li, Xiangang Wan, Wei Chen, and D. Y. Xing
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Miniband Generation by Surface Acoustic Waves
item.description:
Author(s): Eli Meril, Unmesh Ghorai, Tobias Holder, and Rafi Bistritzer

We introduce a new class of tunable periodic structures formed by launching two obliquely propagating surface acoustic waves on a piezoelectric substrate that supports a two-dimensional material. The resulting acoustoelectric superlattice exhibits two salient features. First, its periodicity is wide…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106403] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Eli Meril, Unmesh Ghorai, Tobias Holder, and Rafi Bistritzer

We introduce a new class of tunable periodic structures formed by launching two obliquely propagating surface acoustic waves on a piezoelectric substrate that supports a two-dimensional material. The resulting acoustoelectric superlattice exhibits two salient features. First, its periodicity is wide…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106403] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Miniband Generation by Surface Acoustic Waves
item.dc:creator: Eli Meril, Unmesh Ghorai, Tobias Holder, and Rafi Bistritzer
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Kekulé Order from Diffuse Nesting near Higher-Order Van Hove Points
item.description:
Author(s): Jonas Beck, Jonathan Bodky, Matteo Dürrnagel, Ronny Thomale, Julian Ingham, Lennart Klebl, and Hendrik Hohmann

Translation symmetry-breaking order is assumed to be suppressed by the lack of Fermi surface nesting near certain higher-order Van Hove singularities (HOVHS). We show that the anisotropic band-flattening inherent to such HOVHS, combined with broadening of the Fermi surface due to elevated critical s…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106503] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jonas Beck, Jonathan Bodky, Matteo Dürrnagel, Ronny Thomale, Julian Ingham, Lennart Klebl, and Hendrik Hohmann

Translation symmetry-breaking order is assumed to be suppressed by the lack of Fermi surface nesting near certain higher-order Van Hove singularities (HOVHS). We show that the anisotropic band-flattening inherent to such HOVHS, combined with broadening of the Fermi surface due to elevated critical s…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106503] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Kekulé Order from Diffuse Nesting near Higher-Order Van Hove Points
item.dc:creator: Jonas Beck, Jonathan Bodky, Matteo Dürrnagel, Ronny Thomale, Julian Ingham, Lennart Klebl, and Hendrik Hohmann
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Dual Spin Excitation Components in ${\mathrm{FeSe}}_{0.67}{\mathrm{Te}}_{0.33}$
item.description:
Author(s): Hao Zhang, Hongliang Wo, Yimeng Gu, Zeyu Kao, Gaofeng Ding, Kazuki Iida, Kazuhiko Ikeuchi, and Jun Zhao

Iron chalcogenide superconductors ${\text{FeSe}}_{1−x}{\mathrm{Ch}}_{x}$ ($\mathrm{Ch}=\mathrm{S},\mathrm{Te}$) exhibit an unusual double-dome superconducting phase diagram, the microscopic origin of which remains unclear. Here, we use inelastic neutron scattering to probe spin excitations in single…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106504] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hao Zhang, Hongliang Wo, Yimeng Gu, Zeyu Kao, Gaofeng Ding, Kazuki Iida, Kazuhiko Ikeuchi, and Jun Zhao

Iron chalcogenide superconductors FeSe1−xChx (Ch=S,Te) exhibit an unusual double-dome superconducting phase diagram, the microscopic origin of which remains unclear. Here, we use inelastic neutron scattering to probe spin excitations in single-crystalline FeSe0.67Te0.33, positioned at the supercondu…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106504] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Dual Spin Excitation Components in ${\mathrm{FeSe}}_{0.67}{\mathrm{Te}}_{0.33}$
item.dc:creator: Hao Zhang, Hongliang Wo, Yimeng Gu, Zeyu Kao, Gaofeng Ding, Kazuki Iida, Kazuhiko Ikeuchi, and Jun Zhao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Topological Excitonic Insulators in Electron Bilayers Modulated by Twisted Hexagonal Boron Nitride
item.description:
Author(s): Yongxin Zeng, Allan H. MacDonald, and Nemin Wei

Equilibrium interlayer exciton condensation is common in bilayer quantum Hall systems and is characterized by spontaneous phase coherence between isolated layers. It has been predicted that similar physics can occur in the absence of a magnetic field in some two-dimensional semiconductor bilayers. I…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106602] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yongxin Zeng, Allan H. MacDonald, and Nemin Wei

Equilibrium interlayer exciton condensation is common in bilayer quantum Hall systems and is characterized by spontaneous phase coherence between isolated layers. It has been predicted that similar physics can occur in the absence of a magnetic field in some two-dimensional semiconductor bilayers. I…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106602] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological Excitonic Insulators in Electron Bilayers Modulated by Twisted Hexagonal Boron Nitride
item.dc:creator: Yongxin Zeng, Allan H. MacDonald, and Nemin Wei
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Anyon Superconductivity and Plateau Transitions in Doped Fractional Quantum Anomalous Hall Insulators
item.description:
Author(s): Pavel A. Nosov, Zhaoyu Han, and Eslam Khalaf

A unified theory explains the recent discovery of superconductivity and reentrant integer quantum anomalous Hall states in twisted MoTe2 in terms of the disorder-broadened Landau-Hofstadter bands of doped anyons.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106501] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pavel A. Nosov, Zhaoyu Han, and Eslam Khalaf

A unified theory explains the recent discovery of superconductivity and reentrant integer quantum anomalous Hall states in twisted MoTe2 in terms of the disorder-broadened Landau-Hofstadter bands of doped anyons.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106501] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Anyon Superconductivity and Plateau Transitions in Doped Fractional Quantum Anomalous Hall Insulators
item.dc:creator: Pavel A. Nosov, Zhaoyu Han, and Eslam Khalaf
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Dimensionality-Dependent Exciton Dispersion in a Single-Band Mott Insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Zhibin Su, Junjian Mi, Shaohua Yan, Jiade Li, Siwei Xue, Zhiyu Tao, Enling Wang, Xiongfei Shi, Hechang Lei, Zhuan Xu, Jiandong Guo, and Xuetao Zhu

High-resolution electron scattering confirms the dimensional transition of exciton dispersion from parabolic (3D) to massless linear (2D) behavior near the Brillouin zone center.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106502] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhibin Su, Junjian Mi, Shaohua Yan, Jiade Li, Siwei Xue, Zhiyu Tao, Enling Wang, Xiongfei Shi, Hechang Lei, Zhuan Xu, Jiandong Guo, and Xuetao Zhu

High-resolution electron scattering confirms the dimensional transition of exciton dispersion from parabolic (3D) to massless linear (2D) behavior near the Brillouin zone center.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106502] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Dimensionality-Dependent Exciton Dispersion in a Single-Band Mott Insulator
item.dc:creator: Zhibin Su, Junjian Mi, Shaohua Yan, Jiade Li, Siwei Xue, Zhiyu Tao, Enling Wang, Xiongfei Shi, Hechang Lei, Zhuan Xu, Jiandong Guo, and Xuetao Zhu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Condensed Spin Excitation of Quantized Dirac Fermions in the Quasi-Two-Dimensional Semimetal ${\mathrm{BaMnBi}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Masashi Kumazaki, Azimjon Temurjonov, Takaaki Jinno, Yukihiro Watanabe, Taku Matsuhita, Yoshiaki Kobayashi, and Yasuhiro Shimizu

Dirac semimetals provide a new platform for the quantum Hall effect at low magnetic fields. In the presence of strong spin-orbit coupling, a spin-split Landau level is expected to enhance the bulk quasiparticle excitation. Here we report NMR spectroscopy that site selectively probes dynamic spin sus…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106601] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Masashi Kumazaki, Azimjon Temurjonov, Takaaki Jinno, Yukihiro Watanabe, Taku Matsuhita, Yoshiaki Kobayashi, and Yasuhiro Shimizu

Dirac semimetals provide a new platform for the quantum Hall effect at low magnetic fields. In the presence of strong spin-orbit coupling, a spin-split Landau level is expected to enhance the bulk quasiparticle excitation. Here we report NMR spectroscopy that site selectively probes dynamic spin sus…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106601] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Condensed Spin Excitation of Quantized Dirac Fermions in the Quasi-Two-Dimensional Semimetal ${\mathrm{BaMnBi}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Masashi Kumazaki, Azimjon Temurjonov, Takaaki Jinno, Yukihiro Watanabe, Taku Matsuhita, Yoshiaki Kobayashi, and Yasuhiro Shimizu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Photonic Cyclic Orbit Bound to a Single Weyl Point
item.description:
Author(s): Zhongfu Li, Qingyang Mo, Oubo You, Qingdong Yang, Shaojie Ma, Xinhua Wen, Yuanjiang Xiang, and Shuang Zhang

Weyl orbits are topologically protected cyclotron trajectories that connect Weyl nodes of opposite chiralities in momentum space, coupling bulk chiral Landau levels and surface Fermi arcs to produce quantum oscillations under static magnetic fields. While well-studied in condensed matter systems, th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 103801] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhongfu Li, Qingyang Mo, Oubo You, Qingdong Yang, Shaojie Ma, Xinhua Wen, Yuanjiang Xiang, and Shuang Zhang

Weyl orbits are topologically protected cyclotron trajectories that connect Weyl nodes of opposite chiralities in momentum space, coupling bulk chiral Landau levels and surface Fermi arcs to produce quantum oscillations under static magnetic fields. While well-studied in condensed matter systems, th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 103801] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Photonic Cyclic Orbit Bound to a Single Weyl Point
item.dc:creator: Zhongfu Li, Qingyang Mo, Oubo You, Qingdong Yang, Shaojie Ma, Xinhua Wen, Yuanjiang Xiang, and Shuang Zhang

item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Observation of Quasisteady Dark Excitons and Gapped State in a Doped Semiconductor
item.description:
Author(s): Shangkun Mo, Yunfei Bai, Chunlong Wu, Xingxia Cui, Guangqiang Mei, Qiang Wan, Renzhe Li, Cao Peng, Keming Zhao, Dingkun Qin, Shuming Yu, Hao Zhong, Xingzhe Wang, Enting Li, Yiwei Li, Limin Cao, Min Feng, Sheng Meng, and Nan Xu

Excitons underpin optical phenomena and can intertwine with correlated electronic states. Unlike bright excitons, dark excitons evade conventional optical detection, and their impact on quasiequilibrium electronic structures remains largely unexplored. Here, using angle-resolved photoemission spectr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106401] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shangkun Mo, Yunfei Bai, Chunlong Wu, Xingxia Cui, Guangqiang Mei, Qiang Wan, Renzhe Li, Cao Peng, Keming Zhao, Dingkun Qin, Shuming Yu, Hao Zhong, Xingzhe Wang, Enting Li, Yiwei Li, Limin Cao, Min Feng, Sheng Meng, and Nan Xu

Excitons underpin optical phenomena and can intertwine with correlated electronic states. Unlike bright excitons, dark excitons evade conventional optical detection, and their impact on quasiequilibrium electronic structures remains largely unexplored. Here, using angle-resolved photoemission spectr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106401] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Observation of Quasisteady Dark Excitons and Gapped State in a Doped Semiconductor
item.dc:creator: Shangkun Mo, Yunfei Bai, Chunlong Wu, Xingxia Cui, Guangqiang Mei, Qiang Wan, Renzhe Li, Cao Peng, Keming Zhao, Dingkun Qin, Shuming Yu, Hao Zhong, Xingzhe Wang, Enting Li, Yiwei Li, Limin Cao, Min Feng, Sheng Meng, and Nan Xu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Disentangling Electronic and Ionic Nonlinear Polarization Effects in Bulk THz Kerr Response
item.description:
Author(s): Chao Shen, Maximilian Frenzel, Sebastian F. Maehrlein, and Zhanybek Alpichshev

Terahertz (THz) spectroscopy is a powerful probe of low-energy excitations in complex materials. Extending it into the nonlinear regime broadens its scope and can provide valuable insight into interactions among these modes. However, interpreting nonlinear spectra is challenging because resonant fea…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106901] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chao Shen, Maximilian Frenzel, Sebastian F. Maehrlein, and Zhanybek Alpichshev

Terahertz (THz) spectroscopy is a powerful probe of low-energy excitations in complex materials. Extending it into the nonlinear regime broadens its scope and can provide valuable insight into interactions among these modes. However, interpreting nonlinear spectra is challenging because resonant fea…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106901] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Disentangling Electronic and Ionic Nonlinear Polarization Effects in Bulk THz Kerr Response
item.dc:creator: Chao Shen, Maximilian Frenzel, Sebastian F. Maehrlein, and Zhanybek Alpichshev

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Giant Helicity-Dependent Second Harmonic Generation in Type-2 Weyl Semimetal
item.description:
Author(s): Aindrila Sinha, K. P. Mithun, D. V. S. Muthu, and A. K. Sood

Second harmonic generation (SHG) has emerged as a powerful probe of fundamental geometric and topological attributes of the band structure of the Weyl semimetals (WSMs) through the photocurrent generation (shift, injection, and anomalous currents). Notably, circular photocurrents, such as injection …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106902] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Aindrila Sinha, K. P. Mithun, D. V. S. Muthu, and A. K. Sood

Second harmonic generation (SHG) has emerged as a powerful probe of fundamental geometric and topological attributes of the band structure of the Weyl semimetals (WSMs) through the photocurrent generation (shift, injection, and anomalous currents). Notably, circular photocurrents, such as injection …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106902] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Giant Helicity-Dependent Second Harmonic Generation in Type-2 Weyl Semimetal
item.dc:creator: Aindrila Sinha, K. P. Mithun, D. V. S. Muthu, and A. K. Sood

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Brightening Interlayer Excitons by Electric-Field-Driven Hole Transfer in Bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Tianyi Ouyang, Erfu Liu, Soonyoung Cha, Rukai Cang, Raj Kumar Paudel, Yiyang Sun, Zhaoran Xu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Nathaniel M. Gabor, Yia-Chung Chang, and Chun Hung Lui

Bilayer WSe2 hosts bright interlayer A and B excitons even when separated by ~300 meV from intralayer excitons, a regime where conventional exciton hybridization models fail.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096903] Published Fri Mar 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tianyi Ouyang, Erfu Liu, Soonyoung Cha, Rukai Cang, Raj Kumar Paudel, Yiyang Sun, Zhaoran Xu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Nathaniel M. Gabor, Yia-Chung Chang, and Chun Hung Lui

Bilayer WSe2 hosts bright interlayer A and B excitons even when separated by ~300 meV from intralayer excitons, a regime where conventional exciton hybridization models fail.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096903] Published Fri Mar 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Brightening Interlayer Excitons by Electric-Field-Driven Hole Transfer in Bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Tianyi Ouyang, Erfu Liu, Soonyoung Cha, Rukai Cang, Raj Kumar Paudel, Yiyang Sun, Zhaoran Xu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Nathaniel M. Gabor, Yia-Chung Chang, and Chun Hung Lui

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum Mpemba Effect in Long-Range Spin Systems
item.description:
Author(s): Shion Yamashika and Filiberto Ares

One of the manifestations of the quantum Mpemba effect (QME) is that a tilted ferromagnet exhibits faster restoration of the spin-rotational symmetry after a quantum quench when starting from a larger tilt angle. This phenomenon has recently been observed experimentally in an ion trap that simulates…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 090402] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shion Yamashika and Filiberto Ares

One of the manifestations of the quantum Mpemba effect (QME) is that a tilted ferromagnet exhibits faster restoration of the spin-rotational symmetry after a quantum quench when starting from a larger tilt angle. This phenomenon has recently been observed experimentally in an ion trap that simulates…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 090402] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Mpemba Effect in Long-Range Spin Systems
item.dc:creator: Shion Yamashika and Filiberto Ares
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Symmetry and Topology of Successive Quantum Feedback Control
item.description:
Author(s): Junxuan Wen, Zongping Gong, and Takahiro Sagawa

We establish a symmetry classification for a general class of quantum feedback control. For successive feedback control with a nonadaptive sequence of bare measurements (i.e., with positive Kraus operators), we prove that the symmetry classification collapses to the ten-fold ${\mathrm{AZ}}^{†}$ clas…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 090802] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Junxuan Wen, Zongping Gong, and Takahiro Sagawa

We establish a symmetry classification for a general class of quantum feedback control. For successive feedback control with a nonadaptive sequence of bare measurements (i.e., with positive Kraus operators), we prove that the symmetry classification collapses to the ten-fold AZ† classes, specifying …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 090802] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Symmetry and Topology of Successive Quantum Feedback Control
item.dc:creator: Junxuan Wen, Zongping Gong, and Takahiro Sagawa
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Driven-Dissipative Landau Polaritons: Two Highly Nonlinearly Coupled Quantum Harmonic Oscillators
item.description:
Author(s): Farokh Mivehvar

Landau levels (LLs) are the massively degenerate discrete energy spectra of charged particles in a transverse magnetic field, and they lie at the heart of many intriguing phenomena, such as the integer and fractional quantum Hall effects as well as quantized vortices. In this Letter, we consider cou…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 093602] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Farokh Mivehvar

Landau levels (LLs) are the massively degenerate discrete energy spectra of charged particles in a transverse magnetic field, and they lie at the heart of many intriguing phenomena, such as the integer and fractional quantum Hall effects as well as quantized vortices. In this Letter, we consider cou…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 093602] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Driven-Dissipative Landau Polaritons: Two Highly Nonlinearly Coupled Quantum Harmonic Oscillators
item.dc:creator: Farokh Mivehvar
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Competing and Intertwined Orders in Boson-Doped Mott Antiferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Xin Lu, Jia-Xin Zhang, Lukas Homeier, Shou-Shu Gong, D. N. Sheng, and Zheng-Yu Weng

Inspired by the recent experimental advances in cold atom quantum simulators, we explore the experimentally implemented bosonic $t–{t}^{′}–J$ model on the square lattice using large-scale density matrix renormalization group simulations. By tuning the doping level $δ$ and hopping ratio ${t}^{′}/t$, …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096506] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xin Lu, Jia-Xin Zhang, Lukas Homeier, Shou-Shu Gong, D. N. Sheng, and Zheng-Yu Weng

Inspired by the recent experimental advances in cold atom quantum simulators, we explore the experimentally implemented bosonic t–t′–J model on the square lattice using large-scale density matrix renormalization group simulations. By tuning the doping level δ and hopping ratio t′/t, we uncover six d…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096506] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Competing and Intertwined Orders in Boson-Doped Mott Antiferromagnets
item.dc:creator: Xin Lu, Jia-Xin Zhang, Lukas Homeier, Shou-Shu Gong, D. N. Sheng, and Zheng-Yu Weng
